title: Cat BA Week 1 Analysis
Cat BA Week 1 Analysis
Cat BA Week 1 Analysis
Cat BA Week 1 Analysis
code: growth <- fit_poisson_growth(inc) growth$estimates #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   term  growth_rate std_error conf_low conf_high doubling_time #>   <chr>       <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1 day         0.109    0.0196   0.0709     0.148          6.34

title: Cat BA Week 1 Analysis
code: si <- c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.25, 0.15) renewal_tbl <- renewal_expected_incidence(   incidence = inc$incidence,   rt = 1.2,   serial_interval = si ) head(renewal_tbl) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>     day infectiousness    rt expected_incidence #>   <int>          <dbl> <dbl>              <dbl> #> 1     1           0      1.2               0    #> 2     2           0.1    1.2               0.12 #> 3     3           0.2    1.2               0.24 #> 4     4           0.4    1.2               0.48 #> 5     5           0.85   1.2               1.02 #> 6     6           1.35   1.2               1.62

title: Cat BA Week 1 Analysis
code: sims <- simulate_poisson_process(renewal_tbl$expected_incidence, n_sims = 3, seed = 42) head(sims) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>     sim   day expected_incidence incidence #>   <int> <int>              <dbl>     <int> #> 1     1     1               0            0 #> 2     1     2               0.12         1 #> 3     1     3               0.24         1 #> 4     1     4               0.48         0 #> 5     1     5               1.02         2 #> 6     1     6               1.62         2

title: Cat BA Week 1 Analysis
code: outbreak_consensus_summary(   incidence_data = inc,   rt_estimates = tibble::tibble(),   growth_fit = growth ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>   start_date end_date   days_observed total_incidence peak_date  peak_incidence #>   <date>     <date>             <int>           <dbl> <date>              <dbl> #> 1 2024-01-01 2024-01-23            23              79 2024-01-23             12 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: latest_rt <dbl>, growth_rate <dbl>, doubling_time <dbl>, #> #   trend <chr>, best_model <chr>

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kana Contributor. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Contributor K (2026). omia: Epidemiological Statistical Workflows Outbreak Analysis. R package version 0.1.0, https://example.org/omia.
code: @Manual{,   title = {omia: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows for Outbreak Analysis},   author = {Kana Contributor},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://example.org/omia}, }

what: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows for Outbreak Analysis
title: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows for Outbreak Analysis
text: omia R package epidemiological/statistical outbreak workflows, including: incidence data preparation time-varying Rt estimation (EpiEstim wrapper) renewal-equation expected incidence calculations Poisson process simulation Poisson growth model fitting doubling time model comparison using AIC/BIC (optional LOOIC) epidemic Rt visualization outbreak consensus summaries

title: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows for Outbreak Analysis
Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows for Outbreak Analysis
code: library(omia)  data(cat_ba_week1)  inc <- prep_incidence_data(cat_ba_week1) growth <- fit_poisson_growth(inc) summary_tbl <- outbreak_consensus_summary(   incidence_data = inc,   rt_estimates = tibble::tibble(),   growth_fit = growth )  summary_tbl

title: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows for Outbreak Analysis
text: functions use optional packages available: estimate_rt_epiestim() requires EpiEstim. compare_incidence_models(include_looic = TRUE) can use loo.

what: Cat BA week 1 incidence dataset — cat_ba_week1
title: Cat BA week 1 incidence dataset — cat_ba_week1
text: Synthetic daily incidence data 23 days used package examples vignette workflows.

title: Cat BA week 1 incidence dataset — cat_ba_week1
Cat BA week 1 incidence dataset — cat_ba_week1
text: tibble 23 rows 3 columns: day Integer day index (1-23). date Date corresponding day. incidence Daily incidence count.

title: Cat BA week 1 incidence dataset — cat_ba_week1
text: See data-raw/provenance.md.

title: Compare incidence model fits — compare_incidence_models
text: Compares fitted models using AIC/BIC optionally LOOIC available.

title: Compare incidence model fits — compare_incidence_models
Compare incidence model fits — compare_incidence_models
text: models named list fitted model objects. include_looic TRUE, attempts compute LOOIC using loo::loo() model log-likelihood draws available.

title: Compare incidence model fits — compare_incidence_models
text: tibble one row per model comparison metrics.

what: Estimate time-varying reproduction number with EpiEstim — estimate_rt_epiestim
title: Estimate time-varying reproduction number with EpiEstim — estimate_rt_epiestim
text: Wrapper around EpiEstim::estimate_R() input validation tidy return format.

title: Estimate time-varying reproduction number with EpiEstim — estimate_rt_epiestim
code: estimate_rt_epiestim(   data,   date_col = "date",   count_col = "incidence",   method = "parametric_si",   config = NULL,   ... )

title: Estimate time-varying reproduction number with EpiEstim — estimate_rt_epiestim
text: data data frame date incidence columns. date_col Name date column data. count_col Name incidence column data. method Method passed EpiEstim::estimate_R(). config Optional EpiEstim configuration list. NULL, default parametric serial interval used. ... Additional arguments passed EpiEstim::estimate_R().

title: Estimate time-varying reproduction number with EpiEstim — estimate_rt_epiestim
text: tibble containing time window indices, dates, R_t estimates.

what: Fit a log-linear Poisson growth model — fit_poisson_growth
title: Fit a log-linear Poisson growth model — fit_poisson_growth
text: Fits Poisson regression incidence time reports growth rate, confidence interval, doubling time.

title: Fit a log-linear Poisson growth model — fit_poisson_growth
Fit a log-linear Poisson growth model — fit_poisson_growth
text: data data frame date incidence columns. date_col Name date column. count_col Name incidence/count column.

title: Fit a log-linear Poisson growth model — fit_poisson_growth
text: list model object, coefficient summary tibble, fitted values tibble.

what: omia: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows — omia
title: omia: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows — omia
text: omia package provides practical tools incidence data preparation, time-varying reproduction number estimation, growth model fitting, model comparison, simulation, reporting.

title: omia: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows — omia
text: Maintainer: Kana Contributor kana@example.com

title: Create outbreak consensus summary — outbreak_consensus_summary
text: Summarizes key outbreak indicators incidence, Rt estimates, growth model output.

title: Create outbreak consensus summary — outbreak_consensus_summary
code: outbreak_consensus_summary(   incidence_data,   rt_estimates,   growth_fit,   model_comparison = NULL,   date_col = "date",   count_col = "incidence" )

title: Create outbreak consensus summary — outbreak_consensus_summary
text: incidence_data Data frame incidence time series. rt_estimates Rt estimates tibble (e.g. estimate_rt_epiestim()). growth_fit Output list fit_poisson_growth(). model_comparison Optional output compare_incidence_models(). date_col Name date column incidence_data. count_col Name incidence column incidence_data.

title: Create outbreak consensus summary — outbreak_consensus_summary
text: one-row tibble high-level outbreak metrics.

title: Plot an epidemic curve — plot_epicurve
text: Creates bar chart incidence time.

title: Plot an epidemic curve — plot_epicurve
Plot an epidemic curve — plot_epicurve
text: data data frame date incidence columns. date_col Name date column. count_col Name incidence column. fill Fill color bars.

title: Plot an epidemic curve — plot_epicurve
text: ggplot2 object.

what: Plot time-varying reproduction number estimates — plot_rt_estimates
title: Plot time-varying reproduction number estimates — plot_rt_estimates
text: Plots mean R_t 95% uncertainty band.

title: Plot time-varying reproduction number estimates — plot_rt_estimates
code: plot_rt_estimates(   rt_data,   date_col = "date_end",   mean_col = "mean_r",   lower_col = "lower_95",   upper_col = "upper_95" )

title: Plot time-varying reproduction number estimates — plot_rt_estimates
text: rt_data tibble, typically estimate_rt_epiestim(). date_col Name date column. mean_col Name mean Rt column. lower_col Name lower interval column. upper_col Name upper interval column.

title: Plot time-varying reproduction number estimates — plot_rt_estimates
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Prepare incidence data — prep_incidence_data
text: Standardizes validates daily incidence data downstream analysis.

title: Prepare incidence data — prep_incidence_data
code: prep_incidence_data(   data,   date_col = "date",   count_col = "incidence",   complete_dates = TRUE,   fill_value = 0 )

title: Prepare incidence data — prep_incidence_data
text: data data frame containing least date incidence column. date_col Name date column. count_col Name incidence/count column. complete_dates TRUE, fill missing dates fill_value. fill_value Value used missing dates complete_dates = TRUE.

title: Prepare incidence data — prep_incidence_data
text: tibble columns date incidence.

what: Compute expected incidence under a renewal equation — renewal_expected_incidence
title: Compute expected incidence under a renewal equation — renewal_expected_incidence
text: Calculates expected incidence historical incidence, time-varying reproduction number, serial interval distribution.

title: Compute expected incidence under a renewal equation — renewal_expected_incidence
Compute expected incidence under a renewal equation — renewal_expected_incidence
text: incidence Numeric vector observed incidence. rt Numeric vector reproduction numbers length incidence length 1. serial_interval Numeric vector serial interval probabilities.

title: Compute expected incidence under a renewal equation — renewal_expected_incidence
text: tibble day index, infectiousness, rt, expected_incidence.

what: Simulate incidence from a Poisson process — simulate_poisson_process
title: Simulate incidence from a Poisson process — simulate_poisson_process
text: Draws Poisson realizations given expected incidence values.

title: Simulate incidence from a Poisson process — simulate_poisson_process
Simulate incidence from a Poisson process — simulate_poisson_process
text: expected_incidence Numeric vector expected incidence values. n_sims Number simulations. seed Optional random seed.

title: Simulate incidence from a Poisson process — simulate_poisson_process
text: tibble simulation id, day index, expected incidence, simulated incidence.
